_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: MLB Gameday: Yankees 4, Red Sox 1 final at Fenway
items.snark: Two games, five runs scored, one on the board tonight, this lineup's cold streak deeper than the snow in Mad River Glen.

items.headline: OTM Fan Post: What's the least enjoyable Red Sox team of your lifetime?
items.snark: Poll the bar, the answer is the one currently making us watch it with the sound off.

items.headline: B/R: New Alex Cora hot seat rumors amid Red Sox struggles
items.snark: It's April 22 and we're already booking Cora's exit interview, the hot-seat industrial complex doing more overtime than the Sox bullpen.

items.headline: NESN: Two players Red Sox got in Devers deal now thriving in new organizations
items.snark: Everyone in the Devers trade is thriving, except the one team that was supposed to.

items.headline: Heavy: Breslow preaches patience, '12-game stretch feels kind of arbitrary'
items.snark: Imagine grabbing the mic at 9-14 and telling the room to chill out, Breslow's got the same read on the crowd as the DJ at last call.

items.headline: Talk Sox: Eight months later, the Rafael Devers trade tree is rotting
items.snark: Breslow kept the receipts, turns out they were coupons for a store that closed in November.

items.headline: BSJ: Rivera promoted in surprise call-up, Kutter Crawford still shut down
items.snark: 'Crawford remains shut down' is right up there with 'structural decline' on the Sox-Speak Mount Rushmore.

items.headline: Globe: Red Sox call up lefty Eduardo Rivera, has never pitched above Double-A
items.snark: Answering a rotation crisis with a 22-year-old and two Portland starts, Breslow drafting from the back booth at last call.

items.headline: Bosox Injection: Breslow's biggest 2026 mistake might be a trade he didn't make
items.snark: Twenty-three games in and we're relitigating the offseason, nothing unites this fanbase like prosecuting a trade that never happened.

items.headline: MLBTR: Red Sox select Rivera, option Anderson, Casas to 60-day IL
items.snark: Skipped Triple-A entirely, meaning either the kid is that good or Worcester is that bad. Not mutually exclusive.

items.headline: OTM: Thanks to MLB's expanded playoffs, the Sox still aren't out of it
items.snark: The cope pipeline has us comparing this club to the '51 Giants, I'll drink to that and three more.

items.headline: MLBTR: Red Sox place Sonny Gray on 15-day IL with hamstring strain
items.snark: Staff ace's hamstring grabbed him harder than a Sox fan grabbing the railing leaving Tasty Burger at 2am. Samaniego up, vibes down.

items.headline: Globe: Time for Payton Tolle? Rotation spot open with Gray on IL
items.snark: Nothing says 'we had a plan' like the top prospect arriving because every arm on the big club snapped. Let's go kid, everyone else is broken.

items.headline: 98.5 column: The Red Sox are caught in the middle of nowhere
items.snark: Not tanking, not contending, just sitting there like a guy nursing one beer for three hours and taking up a whole booth.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit links: NFL Draft a fountain of youth and depth for Vrabel's build
items.snark: Fountain of youth sounds great until you remember we drank from it in '21 and got Mac.

items.headline: Patriots re-sign fullback Jack Westover
items.snark: Still rostering a fullback in 2026, Vrabel running his offense like a guy who orders a steak at a sushi place and makes it work.

items.headline: 98.5 Mock 3.0: Denzel Boston falls, Patriots pounce for Maye's new WR1
items.snark: Drafting a guy named Boston to play in Boston and his name is Denzel, the marketing team just hit the buzzer at happy hour.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: How Patriots' 2026 draft capital compares to the rest of the league
items.snark: Pats sitting on more capital than a guy at last call with a fresh paycheck, Vrabel about to spend it all at once.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: 6 NFL Draft prospects we absolutely love
items.snark: Nothing says 'our guys' like a bunch of bloggers falling in love 24 hours before they never get drafted there anyway.

items.headline: NBCS Boston Mock 6.0: Patriots trade down, grab Vandy TE Eli Stowers
items.snark: Six mocks deep and NBCS still thinks the Pats are patient enough to trade back and take a tight end. Gronk wept.

items.headline: Lazar's Final Mock: Patriots take A&M Edge Cashius Howell at 31
items.snark: Fourth 'final' mock in six days, at some point Lazar's just going to draft the guy himself and save us the trouble.

items.headline: Schefter: Patriots have 'interest' in ASU OT Max Iheanachor at 31
items.snark: Schefter 'sources' at this point include the guy next to him at the Whole Foods salad bar.

items.headline: Patriots enter draft with 11 picks, tied for 2nd-most in the league
items.snark: Eleven picks and still somehow the league thinks we need a quiet draft, Vrabel's about to turn this into a yard sale.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: Draft prospects the Patriots showed particular interest in
items.snark: Top-30 visit list longer than the line outside Tasty Burger at 2 AM and twice as greasy.

items.headline: CBS Sports: How a post-June 1 AJ Brown deal reshapes the Eagles and the 2026 draft
items.snark: Every cap analyst in America suddenly a wedding planner obsessed with June 1 like it's the only date left on the calendar this year.

items.headline: SI: Mapping every realistic Patriots trade-down scenario
items.snark: Every realistic scenario mapped, the least realistic being 'the Pats stay put and take the best guy left.' Vrabel's the GM version of a guy who has to touch every menu item.

items.headline: Vrabel addresses Boutte's absence from start of offseason program
items.snark: Boutte in Miami instead of Foxboro in April, honestly the most relatable thing a Patriot's done in years.

items.headline: Vrabel non-answer on Brown: 'Continue to try to strengthen our team'
items.snark: Vrabel dodged that question like a guy who just noticed his ex at the same bar. Respectable.
